name: Micaiah and the lying spirit
notes: Kings and Chronicles recount the same episode almost word for word: Jehoshaphat of Judah comes to Ahab of Israel, who persuades him to go up against Ramoth-gilead. Four hundred prophets promise victory, but Micaiah the son of Imlah foretells Israel scattered as sheep without a shepherd and a lying spirit in the mouth of the prophets, and is smitten and imprisoned with bread and water of affliction; Ahab disguises himself yet is struck between the joints of the harness and dies at even. The shared core runs verse for verse from the proposal of war through the king's death. Each account preserves its own vantage in small touches: Kings frames the muster as Ahab speaking first to his servants about Ramoth (1Kgs 22:3) and closes with the burial, the dogs licking the blood, and Ahaziah's succession (1Kgs 22:36-40), while Chronicles opens with Jehoshaphat's affinity with Ahab (2Chr 18:1) and adds at the turning of the captains that Jehoshaphat cried out and the LORD helped him, and God moved them to depart from him (2Chr 18:31). These are complementary vantages on one event.

links.label: Jehoshaphat the king of Judah came down to the king of Israel
Wilt thou go with me ... to Ramoth-gilead ... I am as thou art my people as thy people
Enquire I pray thee at the word of the LORD to day
four hundred men ... Go up for the Lord shall deliver it into the hand of the king
Is there not here a prophet of the LORD besides that we might enquire of him
I hate him for he doth not prophesy good ... but evil ... Let not the king say so
the king of Israel called an officer ... Micaiah the son of Imlah
sat each on his throne ... their robes in a void place in the entrance of the gate of Samaria
Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah made him horns of iron ... With these shalt thou push the Syrians until thou have consumed them
all the prophets prophesied so saying Go up to Ramoth-gilead and prosper
the words of the prophets declare good unto the king ... speak that which is good
As the LORD liveth ... that will I speak
Go and prosper for the LORD shall deliver it into the hand of the king
How many times shall I adjure thee ... nothing but ... true in the name of the LORD
I saw all Israel scattered upon the hills as sheep that have not a shepherd
Did I not tell thee that he would prophesy no good concerning me but evil
I saw the LORD sitting on his throne and all the host of heaven standing by him on his right hand and on his left
Who shall persuade Ahab that he may go up and fall at Ramoth-gilead
there came forth a spirit and stood before the LORD
I will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets ... go forth and do so
the LORD hath put a lying spirit in the mouth of ... these thy prophets and the LORD hath spoken evil
Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah ... smote Micaiah on the cheek ... Which way went the Spirit of the LORD from me
thou shalt see ... when thou shalt go into an inner chamber to hide thyself
Take Micaiah and carry him back unto Amon the governor of the city and to Joash the king's son
Put this fellow in the prison and feed him with bread of affliction and with water of affliction
If thou return at all in peace the LORD hath not spoken by me ... Hearken
the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead
I will disguise myself ... but put thou on thy robes And the king of Israel disguised himself
the king of Syria ... captains ... Fight neither with small nor great save only with the king of Israel
the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat ... and Jehoshaphat cried out
perceived that it was not the king of Israel ... they turned back from pursuing him
a certain man drew a bow at a venture and smote the king of Israel between the joints of the harness
the battle increased that day ... stayed up in his chariot against the Syrians and died at even
